name: Florentine Triangle Jewels
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 15-inch x 10-inch jelly roll pan with foil.
2. Prepare the Buttery Crust by pressing the crust mixture evenly into the bottom of the foil-lined pan. Refrigerate while preparing the topping.
3. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine butter, granulated sugar, and whipping cream. Place over medium heat; cook until the mixture boils, stirring often.
5. Boil for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Stir in candied cherries, candied pineapple, almonds, and orange peel.
6. Spread the mixture evenly over the chilled crust.
7. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden. Allow to cool in the pan.
8. Meanwhile, melt chocolate pieces and drizzle over the baked cookie mixture while it is slightly warm. Allow the chocolate to set.
9. Cut the cooled cookies into 5 lengthwise strips. Then cut each strip into about 13 triangles.
10. Store in the refrigerator if desired.
Buttery Crust:
1. In a food processor fitted with a steel blade, combine flour, powdered sugar, and butter. Process until blended.
2. With the motor running, add vanilla and whipping cream through the feed tube, processing until the dough begins to cling together.
